warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (H₂C=O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard', density-fitted) in the gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF) using a finite-difference three-point scheme on the N, N+1, and N−1 electron systems. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied file (no re-optimization). All three SCFs converged; integrity.trustworthy = true. The headline value reported is the largest f⁺ index: C (atom 2) f⁺ = 0.70839130, identifying the carbonyl carbon as the most electrophilic site (prime target for nucleophilic attack). The oxygen (atom 1) is the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.46742464. Full Fukui table — f⁺: O=0.46814, C=0.70839, H=−0.08827, H=−0.08826; f⁻: O=0.46742, C=0.11244, H=0.21008, H=0.21006; dual descriptor: O=+0.00071, C=+0.59595, H=−0.29835, H=−0.29832. Charge conservation: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000 (both passed). CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp formaldehyde.xyz.
